title: Call SHA256 precompile (0x2) directly via staticcall — cheaper than Solidity sha256()
description: The SHA256 precompile at address 0x2 accepts input bytes and returns 32 bytes. staticcall(gas(), 2, inputOffset, inputLen, outputOffset, 32) runs it. Writing output to 0x01 and using mload(staticcall(...)) reads result in one step since staticcall returns 1 on success. invalid() on failure burns all gas — appropriate for precompile failures which should never happen.
when_to_apply: SHA256 computations — cross-chain proofs, Bitcoin compatibility, any non-keccak hashing need
when_not_to_apply: When keccak256 suffices — SHA256 base cost ~60 gas vs keccak256 ~30 gas
